PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-0 SHIPPED — Export Foundation squash-merged to main (commit 4caa449) and pushed to origin. Five shared helpers + 15 tests live under apps/web/src/server/lib/. SS-1..4 (per-entity Route Handlers + UI) now unblocked. Prior: Item 3 Alert History Log (commit 7da0cab earlier same day).

LAST_DONE:    Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-0 — Export Foundation. Squash-merged from feat/exports-foundation to main (commit 4caa449, pushed origin), feature branch deleted.
              Files added (apps/web/src/server/lib/):
              - `route-auth.ts`: Auth.js v5 session verifier — `requireRouteAuth()` returns `{userId, tenantId, roles}`; throws `RouteAuthError` carrying a 401 NextResponse when session is null or tenant absent. Used by every export Route Handler (security.md L11 — Route Handlers MUST manually verify auth). Plan deviation noted: no `req: NextRequest` parameter (Auth.js v5 reads request context automatically — explicit param would be unused).
              - `export-csv.ts`: ~30-LOC hand-rolled RFC-4180 renderer. UTF-8 BOM for Excel, CRLF line endings, escapes `"`, `,`, `\r`, `\n` via double-quote wrap + internal-quote doubling. Per-column `format?: (v: unknown) => string` callback for custom rendering. `toCsv<T>(rows, columns) → string`.
              - `export-pdf.tsx`: React-PDF Document component. Fixed header (entity name + tenant + ISO timestamp + filter summary), striped table (column headers + alternating row backgrounds), fixed footer (`Page N of M` via render callback). Exports `<ExportPdfDocument {...props}>` JSX component and `renderExportPdf(props): Promise<Buffer>` async wrapper that calls @react-pdf/renderer's `renderToBuffer`.
              - `export-audit.ts`: `writeExportAudit({userId, tenantId, entity, format, filterHash, rowCount})` writes one AuditLog row with `action: "DATA_EXPORT"`, `entityType: <entity>`, `entityId: <filterHash>`, `changesJson: {format, rowCount}`. Matches L5 audit pattern from security.md.
              - `export-filename.ts`: `buildExportFilename(entity, tenantSlug, format, now?)` returns `{entity}-{tenantSlug}-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.{ext}` in UTC. Sortable, tenant-scoped, no-collision.

              Files added (apps/web/src/server/lib/__tests__/):
              - `export-csv.test.ts` — 10 tests: BOM prefix, CRLF endings, header row, plain rows, comma/quote/newline/CR escape, null/undefined → empty cell, format callback application.
              - `export-pdf.test.tsx` — 2 tests: renderExportPdf returns non-empty Buffer starting with `%PDF`; empty-rows fixture still produces a valid PDF.
              - `export-audit.test.ts` — 3 tests: DATA_EXPORT action + identifiers shape, changesJson contains format+rowCount, entityType verbatim. Uses `partial()` helper following alertHistory.test.ts pattern.

              Files modified:
              - `apps/web/package.json`: +`@react-pdf/renderer ^4.5.1` dependency; +`@vitejs/plugin-react` devDependency.
              - `apps/web/pnpm-lock.yaml`: lockfile regen for the two deps + transitive packages (~57 packages added).
              - `apps/web/vitest.config.ts`: added `plugins: [react()]` so vitest can transform JSX in `.tsx` test files. Required because Next.js's tsconfig.base.json sets `jsx: "preserve"` and vite respects that for `.tsx` source files; without the plugin, vite's import-analysis pass fails to parse JSX during test runs. Documented as 🟡 fix in lessons.md.

              Schema/migrations: none — SS-0 is library code only.

              Acceptance gate (all GREEN):
              - typecheck 6/6 packages clean
              - lint 5/5 packages clean
              - tests: 54/54 in apps/web (was 39 — added 15 new: 10 csv + 2 pdf + 3 audit) + 7/7 in packages/jobs (unchanged) = 61 total. TDD order intact — RED first (3 files failed with module-not-found), then implemented, then 15/15 GREEN.
              - `pnpm --filter @marine-guardian/web build` — succeeds. React-PDF SSR compatibility confirmed.

              EXECUTION NOTES:
              Per memory-governance §2.5b and the documented hook-injection thrashing lesson, Sonnet was bypassed up-front. Scope was ~7 source files + 3 test files + 3 governance files. Hook injection still firing (~10× this session) but Opus 4.7 absorbs it cleanly. Token usage well within the 100K Opus ceiling per §2.5b. No retries, no thrashing signals.

              Lint debugging took ~3 iterations: initial 10 errors → 4 errors after source fixes → 2 errors after typed helper → 0 errors after `partial()` adoption. Test-file lints were the long tail (test code is more permissive but project enforces strict on tests too — `partial()` helper from alertHistory.test.ts is the established workaround for `expect.objectContaining` returning `any`).
